narrative.bottleneck: CPython interpreter overhead — every op boxed/unboxed, GIL-bound
narrative.solved: (this is the baseline we improve from)

narrative.did: Replaced loops with broadcasted array ops (mean, std, abs, comparison)
narrative.bottleneck: Interpreter dispatch was 99% of the time, not the math
narrative.solved: One vectorised line replaces three Python loops

narrative.did: JIT-compiled the original Python loops to native machine code
narrative.bottleneck: NumPy creates large temporary arrays for each op (memory bw)
narrative.solved: Loops fuse; one pass over data instead of N passes

narrative.did: Replaced range() with prange() over patients
narrative.bottleneck: Single-threaded — only 1 of 8+ cores in use
narrative.solved: Patients are independent (embarrassingly parallel); all cores busy

narrative.did: Down-cast input from float64 → float32 before the kernel
narrative.bottleneck: Memory bandwidth — 8 bytes/cell x N_cells exceeds L2 cache
narrative.used: float32 halves the working-set; twice as many values per cache line
narrative.solved: Better cache utilisation; clinical precision unaffected (~7 dp)

narrative.did: Compiled a typed .pyx kernel with float64_t[:, :, :] memoryviews
narrative.bottleneck: JIT warmup cost on first call (Numba) — Cython is AOT
narrative.solved: Same near-C performance, but compiled once at install time

narrative.did: Same NumPy logic, but using torch.Tensor on CPU
narrative.bottleneck: NumPy uses generic BLAS; PyTorch uses MKL/Accelerate w/ vectorisation
narrative.used: torch CPU backend (Apple Accelerate framework on macOS)

results.block: 9. Numba CUDA kernel (real GPU - Tesla T4)

narrative.did: Wrote a @cuda.jit kernel: 1 thread per patient, block size 64
narrative.bottleneck: Memory bandwidth + PCIe transfer; kernel itself is microseconds
narrative.used: @cuda.jit + cuda.grid(1) on real Tesla T4 GPU (Colab)
narrative.solved: Massive thread-level parallelism: each patient handled by its own GPU thread

results._note: narrative text in original JSON said 'simulator' but this run was on a real T4 GPU; corrected here for clarity
